$comment: Release registry consumed by `cppgraph status` (update/rebuild advice) and by scripts/install.sh (default checkout target). `latest` is the newest STABLE version tag (null until the first release is cut → installers fall back to the main branch). Each `releases` entry: `version` (matches the git tag, with or without a leading 'v'), `rebuild` (which layer of the index stack adopting it invalidates: "none" = query-code only, the existing store stays valid; "store" = rebuild the SQLite store from the existing .scip, no recompile; "reindex" = re-run scip-clang then rebuild the store), optional one-line `notes`, and an optional `url` to that release's changelog (keep detail in the CHANGELOG/GitHub release, not here). Sort releases oldest->newest. Example entry: {"version": "0.1.0", "rebuild": "reindex", "notes": "reference index format changed", "url": "https://github.com/rakiz/cppgraph/releases/tag/v0.1.0"}.
$comment_scip_clang: `scip_clang` pins the indexer dependency by `version` only (upstream scip-clang release tag; drives the download URL and the source clone in docker/build-scip-clang) plus `rebuild` (impact of adopting a DIFFERENT version, same vocabulary as releases: "reindex" in practice since a new indexer changes the .scip). The *variant* (stock vs enclosing_range-504) is intentionally NOT pinned: they are two valid capability levels, and a graph's variant is independent of the locally installed binary (a #504-indexed graph can be copied to a stock-only machine). `cppgraph status` reports the installed/graph variant for information and advises re-indexing only on a version change; what a graph actually carries (file vs symbol granularity) is surfaced via has_attributed_refs / the usage_view.
